---
title: "Logistic GWAS Visualization"
author: "Kimberly Olney, Ph.D."
date: "March 2026"
output: html_document
---

# Set up

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
.libPaths(c("/home/kolney/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/"))
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, root.dir = ".")

library(data.table)
library(ggmanh)
library(qqman)
library(ggplot2)

# SNP annotation table
snp_anno <- fread("../snp_array/reference/NCBI_SNP_positions_with_gene_hg19ToHg38_lift_over.tsv")
snp_anno[, `:=`(gene = Gene, rsid = names)]

# Mapping from PLINK SNP IDs (CHR:POS:REF:ALT) back to rsIDs
id_map <- fread("../snp_array/reference/CHRPOSREFALT_to_rsid.txt", header = FALSE)
setnames(id_map, c("plink_id", "rsid"))

chr_labels <- c(as.character(1:22))
dir.create("../results/gwas", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

## Define function to process and plot logistic GWAS results
```{r}
process_gwas_logistic <- function(file_path, trait_name, colors, output_prefix) {
  
  # Load GWAS results
  gwas <- fread(file_path)
  
  # Keep only additive SNP test rows
  if ("TEST" %in% colnames(gwas)) {
    gwas <- gwas[TEST == "ADD"]
  }
  
  # Remove rows with missing P-values
  gwas <- gwas[!is.na(P)]
  
  # Map PLINK SNP IDs back to rsIDs
  gwas <- merge(gwas, id_map, by.x = "SNP", by.y = "plink_id", all.x = TRUE)
  
  # Merge with SNP annotation
  gwas_snp <- merge(gwas, snp_anno, by = "rsid", all.x = TRUE)
  
  # Clean chromosome column
  gwas_snp[, CHR := as.character(CHR)]
  gwas_snp <- gwas_snp[CHR %in% chr_labels]
  gwas_snp[, CHR := factor(CHR, levels = chr_labels)]
  
  # Labels for top hits
  gwas_snp[, label := ifelse(P < 1e-5 & !is.na(gene), gene, NA)]
  
  # Manhattan plot
  man_plot <- manhattan_plot(
    x = gwas_snp,
    pval.colname = "P",
    chr.colname = "CHR",
    pos.colname = "BP",
    plot.title = paste("Logistic GWAS", trait_name),
    y.label = "-log10(p-value)",
    chr.col = colors,
    label.colname = "label",
    label.font.size = 2.75,
    highlight.colname = "label",
    highlight.col = "black"
  )
  
  print(man_plot)
  
  ggsave(
    paste0("../results/gwas/manhattan_", output_prefix, ".pdf"),
    plot = man_plot,
    width = 8,
    height = 4.5
  )
  
  # QQ plot
  pdf(paste0("../results/gwas/qq_", output_prefix, ".pdf"), width = 5, height = 5)
  qqman::qq(
    gwas_snp$P,
    main = paste("QQ Plot:", trait_name),
    pch = 18,
    col = colors[1],
    cex = 1.5,
    las = 1
  )
  dev.off()
  
  # Show QQ plot in RMarkdown
  qqman::qq(
    gwas_snp$P,
    main = paste("QQ Plot:", trait_name),
    col = colors[1]
  )
  
  # Save annotated table
  fwrite(
    gwas_snp,
    paste0("../results/gwas/", output_prefix, "_annotated_results.tsv"),
    sep = "\t"
  )
  
  return(gwas_snp)
}
```

## Define logistic GWAS tasks
```{r}
tasks_logistic <- list(
  list(
    "../snp_array/associations_with_imputed_snps/GWAS_DiseaseStatus_Control_vs_Case.assoc.logistic",
    "Disease Status: Control/PA vs AD/LBD",
    c("firebrick4", "salmon2"),
    "DiseaseStatus"
  ),
  list(
    "../snp_array/associations_with_imputed_snps/GWAS_Sex.assoc.logistic",
    "Sex",
    c("darkgreen", "palegreen3"),
    "Sex"
  ),
  list(
    "../snp_array/associations_with_imputed_snps/GWAS_Females_Only.assoc.logistic",
    "Disease Status in Females Only",
    c("deeppink4", "pink2"),
    "FemalesOnly"
  ),
  list(
    "../snp_array/associations_with_imputed_snps/GWAS_Males_Only.assoc.logistic",
    "Disease Status in Males Only",
    c("midnightblue", "lightblue2"),
    "MalesOnly"
  )
)

gwas_logistic_results <- list()

for (task in tasks_logistic) {
  gwas_logistic_results[[task[[4]]]] <- process_gwas_logistic(
    file_path = task[[1]],
    trait_name = task[[2]],
    colors = task[[3]],
    output_prefix = task[[4]]
  )
}
```
